name: Duality
core_insight: Many systems have two complementary descriptions that are each incomplete but together exhaustive. You cannot simultaneously sharpen position and momentum; wave and particle; electric and magnetic — each pair is a dual. Choosing one representation hides the other.

key_equations.name: Wave-Particle Duality
key_equations.why: Particle energy tied to wave frequency; particle momentum to wave wavelength — neither description alone is sufficient

key_equations.name: Position-Momentum Duality
key_equations.why: Heisenberg uncertainty as duality: tighter position means broader momentum spread, and vice versa

key_equations.name: Electric-Magnetic Duality
key_equations.why: Maxwell's equations are symmetric under swapping E and B — same physics, dual description

seed_prompt: ◑ What is the dual of this description? Sharpen one side and the other blurs. Both are real.
